description: The world's best-preserved ancient castle-style city gate, an important part of the Nanjing City Wall.
A double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and city skyline.
The residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style, known as the 'No. 1 Villa in the Far East.'
A Qinhuai River water sightseeing experience, showcasing ancient architecture and night views along the riverbanks, capturing the charm of the Six Dynasties capital.
The site where the Treaty of Nanking was signed, an important witness to modern history.
A commercial district with a Republican-era style, offering dining, entertainment, and shopping, with vibrant night scenes popular among young people.
A Ming Dynasty pavilion-style building, the highest point along the Yangtze River in the area, offering expansive views.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory, Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, providing panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
The ruins of an ancient Southern Dynasty temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A famous food street in Nanjing, featuring a variety of local snacks and night market delicacies, offering an authentic taste of Nanjing.
